title: they declined a small goodwill gesture
name: Paul Nicoll
review: There were several issues, including the onboard service, condition of the lavatory, problems with the moving map and a disappointing breakfas. I contacted Customer Care, and while they responded promptly, I was disappointed that they declined even a small goodwill gesture.

title: "just had to just sleep"
name: Alwaleed Althani
review: Came in on a connecting flight on Cathay Pacific flight from Guangzhou going onto Doha. At 2 AM everything at the airport was closed except for the lounges. Onboard nice QSuites but I just had to just sleep, and had no food or used entertainment.

title: "sandwich no choice"
name: Alwaleed Althani
review: A330 comfortable but very much underwhelming. Paper bags with a sandwich no choice, but as the flight that lasts 45 minutes getting there was more important.

title: "cabin crew was professional and friendly"
name: L Casine
review: I recently flew with Qatar Airways on a Boeing 777-300ER and my experience was mixed overall, so I would rate it 3 stars. The onboard entertainment system was excellent, with a great selection of movies and internet access throughout the flight. The meals and drinks were also very good, and the onboard service from the cabin crew was professional and friendly. However, my seats was quite small and narrow. One of the biggest disappointments was the cabin baggage allowance of only 7kg, andthe extra baggage charges were 276 Chinese RMB (40usd- 35euros) per additional kilogram. The ground staff experience in Guangzhou was very unpleasant and poorly handled

title: "Outstanding experience"
name: K Dhanoop
review: Outstanding experience with Qatar Airways My recent journey was particularly memorable, as it was my second experience flying business class and it exceeded expectations.  Priority check-in eliminated long queues, and lounge access provided a calm, comfortable environment to relax. Onboard, the seat was spacious and converted into a fully flat bed. The service was attentive without being intrusive, and the dining experience stood out as well, thoughtfully presented, with a variety of options. A special mention goes to the cabin crew whose service truly elevated the journey. Highly recommended.

title: "Very nice QSuites"
name: Alwaleed Althani
review: Very nice QSuites, I had 5A which is back facing. Being tired I was not interested in having any food. The WIFI was great throughout the flight except over India. Cabin staff were nice not top notch Qatar Airways standard but still very good.

title: "service was very good"
name: Michael Schade
review: Frankfurt Airport check in was chaotic, it took me over an hour to check in. Flight to Doha was ok, with decent food and good service. Arrival transport in Doha was by remote stand and bus, despite a quiet airport. Al Safwa lounge good as always. Departure was by remote stand and bus and it wasn’t clear when BC was suppose to board as the special bus for BC wasn’t there. Going through this at 3 am contributes to the stress of travelling. Food was ok on the 2nd leg of the journey, service was very good

title: "Boarding in Johannesburg was chaotic"
name: Alwaleed Althani
review: Boarding in Johannesburg was chaotic there should have been two airbridges but only one was utilized and this was on the ground staff in JNB. There was a menu but what was on the menu wasn’t what was catered and I ended up having to settle for a cheese platter of the lowest quality. I think Qatar Airways need to review their ground operations and catering in Johannesburg as I have done this route three times a couple of years ago and had a wonderful experience.

title: "no realistic re-routing options"
name: C Barlanek
review: My flight from Bangkok to Prague on 15 March was canceled due to the Middle East conflict 3 days before departure via email. No alternative was provided other than a flight a week later. ​Zero Communication: I sent 6 emails, tried the hotline repeatedly, used Live Chat, and reached out on X but absolute silence. No offer of accommodation, meals, no realistic re-routing options. I was forced to buy a new ticket with China Airlines at my own expense to get home

title: "a great lie-flat seat"
name: Eelco van den Heuvel
review: By far the cheapest option on short notice between Amsterdam and Bangkok. Paid little over 800 euros for a return. which was a very good price. Got the chance of an upgrade to business class however for a good price and decided to treat myself. From the moment you step on board you feel welcome. Adressed by your name and the stewardesses introduce themselves. Welcome drinks, dine on demand, great food, a great lie-flat seat and the cabin almost felt homely. Second leg equally good. I love the lounge in the A380. Relaxed boarding and out in no time.  Unfortunately my return flight was cancelled 4 times due to the conflict in the Middle East. I must say that I saw a lot of complaints about QR in which the airline was accused of not responding or not being reachable. My experience is different. I had to call them 4 times and I must I got through pretty quick. Yes, off course there is a waiting time in such an extreme situation. They easily booked me on a new flight every time it was cancelled. Since I could work from Thailand, I was not in a big hurry to get a flight home. After the last cancellation I requested to book me on another airline. Which was granted promptly.

title: "experience was not one of our best"
name: Colin Kay
review: My wife and I have flown Qatar many times, but this experience was not one of our best. Hard product was good, although the 777 used on the DOH-MLE-DOH sector must be one of the oldest in their fleet and feels it. Soft product was not up to scratch on the LGW-DOH leg. This was a holiday flight and my wife and I like our wine. The brut champagne ran out before we were an hour into the flight, and the sauvignon blanc ran out before we were half way through the six hour flight.

title: "No compensation offered"
name: R Morsch
review: Bought a Business Class Lite ticket with no lounge access. First flight in QSuites was fine. In Doha our plane to Vie was 4 hours late due to operational reasons. No compensation offered, nothing. Flight itself was ok with a very mediocre lunch.

title: "changed my pre arranged seat"
name: Andrew Buckley
review: Parts of the seat in business class were broken and I wouldn't be able to use the arm rest. They changed my pre arranged seat to another, and when I asked why the ground staff said we can change your seat.

title: "predictably high-quality product"
name: Rajan Parrikar
review: Goa (Mopa) to Doha in Business Class on a Boeing 787-8. The short three-hour flight departed on time. The cabin featured Qatar Airways’ older Business Class configuration rather than the Qsuite, but the seat remained very comfortable. On-board service was efficient and unobtrusive. Overall, a predictably high-quality product from this airline.

title: "my seat was cramped"
name: Rajan Parrikar
review: Doha to Copenhagen in Business Class on a Boeing 787-9. The aircraft, about 6 years old, carried Qatar Airways’ pre-Qsuite Business Class cabin. In theory, still a premium product, but my seat was cramped and uncomfortable. The stewardess was disengaged, showed no warmth, and operated on autopilot, as if merely ticking boxes rather than attending to passengers. This was an unusually poor Qatar Airways Business Class experience.

title: "very impressed with the service"
name: J Keshy
review: It was a fantastic experience from start to finish. The crew was professional, friendly, and helpful. I want to give a special mention to crew member named Denise whose service was outstanding. Additionally, the cabin manager was engaging and ensured everything ran smoothly. I am very impressed with the service and look forward to flying with Qatar Airways again.

title: "excellent service provided"
name: Michael Schade
review: Check in at Frankfurt Terminal 2 fast and friendly. Contract lounge average with poor F/B choices. Boarding not well organized despite having many gate agents on duty. I was asked to take a seat in the separate business class section of the waiting area. The section was already occupied by economy class pax since there was nobody to check the boarding passes at the entrance to the section. The flight itself was uneventful with decent food and drinks with excellent service provided by the chief purser of the flight being in charge of my side of the cabin. He worked tirelessly throughout the night and even explained the different wines; something which I never experienced after 100+ flights on Qatar. It’s unfortunate that I can’t recall his name, but I remember that he was from Malaysia. Arrival in Doha was unfortunately at another bus gate, despite having so many available contact gates. This cut my visit at the Al Safwa lounge short and there was just time to have a quick coffee. The flight to BKK on a full A380 was uneventful with again great service provided. Food on this flight was average. WiFi did not work throughout the flight

title: food choices ran out
name: Jamie King
review: A complete lack of communication when trying to get in touch about baggage that was lost en route. The Chat on WhatsApp hasn't been answered in over 30 hours and the email I sent, not responded to so far. I called customer service who offered no help other than telling me to go to the website and couldn't offer any useful answer. On the flight the service was inconsistent. Twice, the food choices ran out offering only 2 meals on a duration of 17 hours. The in flight entertainment options were good but the seats were on the small side.

title: "elegance, comfort, and hospitality"
name: Iman Yusuf
review: From the moment I stepped aboard my Qatar Airways flight in Djibouti on January 8, 2026, until arrival Milan Malpensa the following day, I was enveloped in a world of elegance, comfort, and hospitality.  The cabin crew on both legs of my journey Djibouti to Doha, and Doha to Milan were nothing short of extraordinary. Their warmth, professionalism, and genuine attentiveness elevated every moment onboard.  Before I could even glance toward my empty glass, a smiling attendant was already offering a refill.  The cuisine was restaurant-quality and paired with premium beverages. Even the snacks between meals were memorable. And when I mentioned how much I enjoyed the chips, they brought me a second chips with a smile.  My stopover in Hamad Airport was magical and transformed my layover into a luxurious interlude. The seamless transit, spotless facilities, and intuitive design made navigating the airport effortless and even enjoyable.
route: Djibouti to Milan via Doha Hamad

title: "Catering was average"
name: Michael Schade
review: Check in, security check and immigration at BKK airport efficient and friendly. Contract lounge at the new Satellite Terminal average. There was an aircraft change from an ex CX aircraft with their premium economy seats, which I booked to an A380. Seat on the small economy section upstairs was quiet and reasonably comfortable. WiFi did not work. Catering was average with no butter, cheese or crackers. Main meal was ok. Service was fast and friendly. Boarding of the last sector at Doha airport was disorganized. After being toldat that the flight was not ready to board at boarding time and to take a seat, boarding started a minute later. Unfortunately, that was just half the case because the aircraft wasn’t ready and everyone had to wait on the airbridge for 20 minutes. The flight was uneventful with the same catering issues as the previous flight. WiFi was patchy, but worked at times. Flight arrived 20 min ahead of schedule. Service was somehow slow on this red eye flight.

title: "like a first class experience"
name: J Calenski
review: As a business class traveller the service with Qatar is excellent. The check in at Heathrow seamless and the lounge offers excellent range of food and drink along with flight announcements. I was fortunate to fly the Qsuite on both legs of the journey. It offers so much privacy and the in demand dining is a bonus. You are made to feel special and to be honest its more like a first class experience. I used Al Maha services to transit it was literally a 10 min transfer from the airbridge to the lounge and when it was time to board I was collected and fast tracked to board my second

title: "deeply disappointing"
name: Nenad Vukusic
review: Due to bad weather, the Abu Dhabi–Doha flight was delayed and then cancelled, causing a missed connection to Zagreb. Weather disruptions happen — that is not my complaint. The issue was the lack of assistance from Qatar Airways. There was no support, no hotel, no transport, no guidance. I was left to handle everything on my own for more than 24 hours at the airport. I am a Qatar Airways Gold member, and this experience was deeply disappointing.

title: "ate and drank all that I wanted"
name: Anders Pedersen

title: at the top of the industry
name: Rajan Parrikar

title: "remains my choice of airline"
name: Mercy Alexander
review: I recently flew with Qatar Airways and had an excellent experience from start to finish. From check-in to arrival, everything felt well-organized and professional. Qatar remains my choice of airline for my home travels as due to convenience, less stops, better service and experience. I flew American from Dallas to Doha and had to pay for my second baggage even as an international passenger and will have to pay for return ticket too. I would request Qatar Airways to advocate for second baggage free when partnering customers who have to fly American and Qatar. That would be a great service to passengers. Boarding was smooth, and the flight itself was calm and well-managed. Small details — like frequent check-ins from the crew and overall comfort — really stood out and made the journey enjoyable. Everyone at Qatar was courteous. I love the airport assistance that is available throughout and would like to send kudos for managing it so efficiently. Overall, Qatar Airways delivered a high-quality travel experience, and I would confidently choose them again and recommend them to others.

title: "quite underwhelming"
name: T Lusillo
review: My experience was quite underwhelming, the segments I took were delayed minimum 1 hour. Forget about oneworld alliance status to access priority boarding or any of the perks partner airlines traditionally give, with Qatar I needed to constantly ask them and they don’t seem to like reciprocating the entitlements. The plane from Melbourne to Doha had seen better days, the cabin was uncomfortable and tired, the toilets were filthy.

title: "remains my airline of choice"
name: Ahmed Almannaei
review: As a frequent traveler and a loyal Privilege Club member, I choose Qatar Airways for the exceptional level of service they provide. From the moment of check-in to arrival, the experience is seamless and well-organized. The cabin crew are highly professional, attentive, and focused on passenger comfort. The on-board product reflects premium quality, with excellent seating comfort, in-flight entertainment, and high standards of hospitality. Hamad International Airport continues to set a global benchmark for efficiency and passenger experience. Qatar Airways’ commitment to service excellence is evident in every journey. Qatar Airways remains my airline of choice, and I highly recommend them to anyone seeking reliability, comfort, and world-class service.

title: "excellent and memorable"
name: Mauro Tostes
route: Sao Paulo to Moscow via Doha

title: "no compensation has been issued"
name: T Kalan
review: Qatar Airways lost my bag and I finally received the bag at my destination in Muscat after 3 days. They requested me to submit several documents that I complied with. It has been over 3 months, no compensation has been issued to me. I have spent countless hours trying to resolve the issue. I have called their customer service call centers many times. I have sent dozens of emails. Nothing has worked so far.

title: "only one meal"
name: G Murtens
review: Small, mediocre meal, no salt and butter provided. On a 6 hour flight only one meal, no pre-landing snack. Wine served in small doses in plastic sparkling wine glasses. No amenity kit. Other than that, the crew was hard working Seats in Boeing 787 have a weird de-reclining behaviour, sometimes making the crew having to push the seats

title: "completely grateful of the gesture"
name: L Kan
review: On Oct 14, 2025, I was scheduled to fly from DOH to BOS with Qatar in business class with my mom. However, I had this extreme pain in my stomach (I later found out to be kidney stone) and during checkin I was told I could not fly as I had to head to the hospital. Since I was in a foreign country, I did not want any major procedures or imaging due to insurance coverage. About 4 hours later, I was discharged from the hospital and headed back to Doha airport to reschedule my alternative flight as my original flight has already taken off. Since I booked the flight using partner airline miles, Qatar told me they could not do anything and instead I need to speak with the partner. Ultimately I did manage to reach the partner airline through online chat and they told me I need to cancel and rebook but since it was a no show since I missed the flight there is nothing they can so. Thinking through even if I was able to cancel, there is absolutely no way I can book last minute tickets using the original miles.  I explained to the Qatar manager my chat with the partner airline and he still said there is nothing they can do but then stepped away to discuss the matter with other employees. After 10 minutes he came back and said your health is the most important thing, just let us know when you would like to fly, tomorrow or any time we will take care of you. urned out there were 2 business class seats available for the following day and we were able to get on. Qatar did not need to do this but they did and I am completely grateful of the gesture and customer service. For those of you who have flown business with Qatar out of Doha know the business class check in area and the employees there willing to help. I’m not sure if I would have gotten the same level of service in economy but I am forever grateful.

title: "give no butter or salt and pepper"
name: P Ferris
review: The only plus was that it had Starlink wifi. Other than that, food came out far less than other airlines, large breaks in-between food services, kids were starving on way home from doha to Brisbane leg. They give no butter or salt and pepper as a standard with food. Having four flights with Qatar on the round trip, each plane staff enforced different rules for the baby bassinet, one staff refused to let child use at all saying he was too big.  They have no courtesy baby strollers at the airport including Doha airport.

title: Service was surprisingly inattentive
name: S Parlin
review: The Q-Suites hard product was excellent—comfortable seating, strong privacy, and a great screen (9/10). Unfortunately my food was largely inedible (dry, overcooked chicken and stale bread), and meal service was confusing, with my presumed lunch entrée served at breakfast and no option to change. Service was surprisingly inattentive, and I had to request basic items like bedding. No additional meal was offered to me on a 15-hour flight. The service lacked the proactive care I expected.

title: "a very comfortable way to travel"
name: G Mayes
route: New York to Nairobi via Doha

title: "most professional people"
name: S Boyle
review: All fights on time. Planes clean, seats comfortable, food and service good. No complaints really, although it seems the menus offer a plethora of items which aren't always available. Found some surprising music gems in their inflight entertainment system. Could not get the wifi to work. I did not have time to check out their lounge in Doha due to the short connection time but the lounge in BKK is quite the comfortable little palace. Really impressed with their ground staff in Doha - absolutely the nicest and most professional people I have come across in my over 50 decades of flying.

title: "missed our connection"
name: R Walderren
review: The flight was initially delayed by four hours, which can happen but there was no communication from the airline. Because of this delay, we missed our connection and had to book a new one. They offered us a transfer 24 hours later, and refused to provide hotel accommodation, meaning our only option was to sleep on the airport floor. Lounge access was denied. We received a $20 voucher for food and drinks, which is nowhere near sufficient to cover 24 hours at an airport.

title: Starlink WiFi service was excellent
name: Alwaleed Althani
review: Seven year old Boeing 777 in OK condition. The  meal options were poor for me and I skipped the dinner and breakfast. The Starlink WiFi service was excellent. I found the internal lighting was poor I had difficulty reading. IFE was a mixed bag, movies were ok tv and games a disaster

title: "a delightful experience"
name: Mahmud Noormohamed
review: What a delightful experience flying Qatar Airways for the first time. Both outbound and inbound legs were on A350-1000 with Q-Suites. Configuration is 1-2-1 with lie flat beds. Separate check in for business class passengers was very welcome. The Q-Suites offer total privacy. Service is impeccable. A welcome cold drink, Arabic coffee, Qatari dates and refreshing towel. Amenities included noise cancelling headset and pajamas. The letter were very soft and comfortable. Dining is par excellence. Angus Beef tenderloin, key lime pie with citrus meringue and assorted bakery rolls. Appetizer consisted of Qatari mezze with pita bread. The IFE options were very good with movies, tv shows, music and live sports. Surprisingly, no live news channels. Ground crew at check in and boarding were all very friendly and welcoming. In flight crew provided excellent service. WiFi via Starlink connected seamlessly and blazing fast from gate to gate.

title: "Lots of kudos to Qatar Airways"
name: Michael Fewster
review: Lots of kudos to Qatar Airways. Because of an airport strike in Rome, my connecting flight to Rome with another airline made the link to the Qatar Airways flight uncertain. I contacted QATAR online to see if I could change my flight to Adelaide so it started in Athens rather than Rome. I was communicating with a real person in real time. This is much better than having to ring a phone where language becomes an issue as does getting through. This can be difficult or impossible when you are overseas. My Qatar Airways  online agent helped me work it out and the booking was changed. All discussions with airlines should be this easy. There was a service fee but it was reasonable in the circumstances. The flight was excellent. Great staff boarding and during the flight. Friendly and professional. We enjoyed the meals and drinks. Better quality wines than we expected in economy. The accompanying salad was surprisingly fresh,- don't know how they do that. And we received window seats with an empty seat in the middle for the DOHA-Adelaide leg.

title: "a great experience
name: D Khalin
review: It was a great experience. Super friendly staff and amazing onboard entertainment. The food was delicious. We boarded on time and the plane took off also on time.

title: Gold membership is useless
name: M Bialoglowy
review: Gold membership is useless, in Doha they let economy board first, then the priority line is opened and I ended up getting on the flight last behind economy, with all storage space used. A seat screen was broken all flight, keep reseting. Staff don't understand as I l said vegetarian, they give me chicken. Staff was rude to me. The lounge food in Singapore is awful; better eat in foodcourt.

title: "Service and food outstanding"
name: Michael Schade
review: Wonderful stay at Al Safwa lounge. Unfortunately a bus gate, but this time well organized. Well maintained Qsuite. Service and food outstanding. This was the best flight I had with Qatar for a long time. The staff was motivated, efficient and was still able to have a friendly chat. WiFi lightning fast throughout the flight. Flight arrived 20 minutes early at Changi airport. I wish all Qatar flights were that good.

title: "operated by an all-economy A321"
name: Wael Taha
review: I recently traveled with Qatar Airways from Medina to Amsterdam via Doha on October 16th, returning the following day. The Medina–Doha flights were operated by an all-economy A321. he absence of a business class option on this route removes both the comfort of traveling in business class and access to the Al Mourjan or Al Safwa lounges in Doha. The seats on this aircraft felt significantly tighter, with smaller legroom, and the cabin felt cramped and uncomfortable. Although my Gold Privilege Club membership granted me access to the Gold Lounge, it was clearly not comparable to the premium lounges that business class passengers enjoy.

title: "Seat could not be adjusted"
name: Michael Schade
review: The cabin power failed 2 hours into the flight and it was announced the power should be restored soon; which never happened until getting to the gate in Doha. The cockpit never addressed the issue. The power failure resulted in no lights, I had to eat with the mobile phone torch lights on and the IFE did not work. My seat could not be adjusted and had to be put into an upright position upon arrival manually. Upon arrival in Doha, I had already an email from Qatar stating that due to the seat failure (the other issues were never mentioned) I would receive as a gesture of good will a compensation of 9000 Avios.  That offer is an insult.

title: no compensation
name: Rolf Köpke
review: The flight and service was excellent, BUT!!! The baggage service was very bad. One of my luggage with fragile contents were damaged. and no compensation. Explanation: "fragile items are transported with utmost care however our liability for such items are limited."

title: "will be forever grateful"
name: J McLean
review: On the day of our flight from Athens there was a public transport strike and we had great difficulty getting to the airport. After receiving a call from Qatar ground staff at Athens airport enquiring where we were, as bag drop was about to close, we explained our situation and quickly received instructions on who to see upon our late arrival at the airport. Qatar staff fast tracked our check in and then escorted us quickly through security and immigration so that we just made the flight. Cannot imagine another airline would go to the lengths Qatar staff did to help us and will be forever grateful in such a stressful situation.

title: "an exceptional experience"
name: Zainab Alhaddawi
review: Munich to Doha, an exceptional experience from start to finish. The cabin crew were professional, attentive, and genuinely welcoming. The seat comfort, inflight dining, and entertainment were all outstanding—true to Qatar Airways’ five-star reputation. The flight was punctual and smooth, making the journey both relaxing and enjoyable. Highly recommended.

title: "I requested this to be refunded"
name: D Leare
review: We had a flight cancelled which resulted in us spending hours trying to reschedule a new flight, not to mention the extra costs involved with additional accommodation and connecting transport changes. On the morning we were due to fly, we did not receive any notification that the flight had been cancelled and I discovered the cancellation through the app. After arriving home from our trip, I emailed in regards to the extra costs we incurred and as we had paid for seat selection (which we didn't receive) I requested this to be refunded. It is now 10 weeks later, 6 emails later and still no reply whatsoever from Qatar. Their customer service is non existent.

title: "a bus ride from the terminal"
name: Alwaleed Althani
review: The flight is in September which is still pretty hot in Qatar and it was a late morning departure, the plane was parked a bus ride from the terminal, the bus ride was hot. The plane was a very old ex Oman Air A330-300 and was showing it’s age, the toilets were tiny and nowhere near the quality of Qatar Airways A320s. I will continue to use Qatar Airways as I know their standards but on this route I will stick to RJ. I am a Platinum Member on since 2017 I was not recognized and overlooked this staff needs a lot more experience. Alsafwa lounge at Doha is the best lounge I have seen the spa has an amazing reflexology massage service.

title: a truly world class airline
name: H Foster
route: London Heathrow to Lusaka via Doha

title: "Mini Qsuite very private and comfortable"
name: Michael Schade
review: Check in fast and friendly, contract Miracle lounge with poor breakfast offerings. Boarding rather unorganised. Mini Qsuite on the 789 very private and comfortable. Service friendly and efficient. WiFi did not work. My food was not very tasty and poor in presentation. The usually top notch Thai dessert ex BKK was coconut ice-cream (with lots of ice crystals) and two half bananas. Connecting gates system through the entertainment system did not work. Arrived on time. Overall an ok flight with poor quality lounge and on board food.

title: "fantastic and extremely comfortable"
name: J Raiz
review: Initially, I was supposed to be traveling with Qatar Airways' partner, but due to unfortunate circumstances, I was rebooked to an earlier trip the next day with inconvenience compensation. Qatar Airways' service has always been exceptional! I have been a loyal client. The flight I took on 31/8/25 was fantastic and extremely comfortable. Thanks to Vanessa, the flight attendant who attended me while on the trip.

title: "I will remain a loyal customer"
name: Iman Yusuf
review: I want to sincerely thank Qatar Airways for their incredible kindness during a very difficult moment. While checking in at Jeddah Airport, I couldn’t afford the extra baggage fees. The supervisor and assistant at the counter showed me compassion I’ll never forget — they allowed me the extra baggage allowance without charging me anything.  This experience showed me that Qatar Airways is not only a world-class airline but also one that truly cares about its passengers beyond business. Because of this, I will remain a loyal customer.

title: causing me to miss my flight
name: Jarryd Domingo

title: "an experience that borders on First"
name: Rajan Parrikar
review: Doha to Copenhagen in Business Class. There is a reason Qatar Airways remains at the top: exceptional comfort in both hard and soft product. This aircraft did not feature the storied Qsuite but the older configuration, which is still among the best in the sky. Service was warm, polished, and attentive. The WiFi was patchy, though with Starlink integration on the horizon one hopes this will soon be resolved. In sum, no airline delivers greater comfort in Business Class. Qatar offers an experience that borders on First.

title: "on-board service impeccable"
name: P Jannir
review: Goa (Mopa airport) to Doha in Business Class A flawless flight. Boarding was smooth and the on-board service impeccable. This aircraft did not feature the celebrated Qsuite, but the Business Class configuration was still extremely comfortable. My only quibble: the paid WiFi was virtually unusable, dropping out with frustrating regularity.

title: "inflight entertainment wasn't working"
name: Roger Langdon
review: Cape Town to Doha my inflight entertainment wasn't working, I could use free wifi. My return flight from Doha to Cape Town it was not working again. After a long layover in Doha fron Bangkok I wasn't in the mood to be on my phone so nothing to watch. After asking the crew twice to check, no inflight entertainment. Bad!!
route: Bangkok to Cape Town via Doha

title: "best airline I’ve experienced"
name: S Bareno
review: This is the best airline I’ve experienced as economy class, and I love the wide range of options of entertainment. Even though we had medium space for legs, they give you a bag on long distance flights) with compression socks, shades for your eyes, ear plugs and a toothbrush /  toothpaste. That kind of hospitality definitely makes them deserve 9th titled best airline!  The ground staff also organized the groups punctually and kindly. Free WiFi!

title: "nothing short of exceptional"
name: D Hergud
review: Flying Business Class with Qatar Airways was nothing short of exceptional. From the moment I boarded, I was impressed by the attention to detail and the warm welcome from the cabin crew. They were genuinely helpful, attentive without being intrusive, and went out of their way to ensure I was comfortable throughout the flight. What really made the experience memorable was the professionalism and kindness of the crew. Whether it was offering assistance with the seat settings, adjusting lighting, or just checking in regularly with a smile, their service felt personal and sincere. Qatar airways has been my regular airline whenever I fly.

title: very disappointed in the qualty of service
name: Ash Hogan

title: "comfort, service, and efficiency"
name: N Pardele
review: My journey on flight QR039 from Doha to Paris was a perfect blend of comfort, service, and efficiency. The check-in process at Hamad International Airport was smooth and well-organized. Friendly staff assisted with swift baggage drop and clear instructions. Boarding was timely and stress-free, with dedicated lanes for different classes and priority passengers. The aircraft was modern and immaculately clean. The seats offered excellent legroom and ergonomics, even in Economy. Adjustable headrests, a plush seat cushion, and a thoughtfully designed tray table added to the in-flight ease. The in-flight meals were restaurant-quality.  Drinks were served frequently and with a smile, and special meal requests were handled flawlessly.  Qatar’s Oryx One system remains one of the best. It had a wide range of new-release movies, documentaries, music, and games. The touch screen was responsive, and the interface was easy to navigate. Onboard Wi-Fi was available and worked well for light browsing and messaging. The cabin crew on QR039 were attentive and professional. From helping with bags to offering water before being asked, their service was personal and polished. The flight departed on time and landed at Paris Charles de Gaulle smoothly. Disembarkation was quick, and baggage arrived without delay, rounding off a flawless travel experience.

title: service was both warm and precise
name: Rajan Parrikar

title: maintains a high standard of service
name: Rajan Parrikar

title: "substantial delay for my trip"
name: S Masikoto
review: On 23 June 2025, I flew from Hong Kong to Doha. The flight was diverted and arrived in Doha later than scheduled due to the Iranian attack on Qatar, and this caused a substantial delay for my trip. When I arrived in Doha, I learned that the connecting flight to Sao Paulo on 24th, QR779, was canceled.  I approached staff at the airport but he was not at all helpful. The only thing he could tell me was to go to the transfer counter. When I went to the transfer counter area, I witnessed pandemonium. I could not figure out how to line up for the transfer desk, and asked another staff and he told me to go to the business class lounge. I followed that advice and went there. A staff there told me to just wait for a few hours and come back because the company is handling the situation and things should go back to normal then. In the end, I had to spend 20 some hours before I can get a new ticket, and another 20-some hours at the airport before getting on the plane to Sao Paulo on 26th, two days later than the original connection flight. Meanwhile Qatar Airways put my luggage to an earlier flight to Sao Paulo, I had a tagging device to learn that my luggage was already in Sao Paulo for some time when I arrived there.
route: Hong Kong to Sao Paulo via Doha

title: "cancelled the connecting flight"
name: H Karish
review: Qatar Airways cancelled the connecting flight on the departure date when I was at the airport to check in for the first flight. So I had to quickly get another flight with another airline at the airport to fly on the same day. They did not contact me to inform that they can only process a refund if I cancel the return flight as well, which I only found out a couple days after I contacted the airline to check on the partial refund for cancelling my departure flights.

title: a good airline and decent value
name: Nigel Williams
route: Bangkok to London Heathrow via Doha

title: "arrived 77 minutes late"
name: Michael Beer
review: Flight from Bali to Doha arrived 8 mins early, flight from Doha to Vienna arrived 77 minutes late. 2 toilets for 130 passengers in economy on the A320. Although Wheel chair was requested I had to walk to the aircraft.

title: "pay attention to details that matter"
name: Theona Knight
review: It is always a pleasure to experience Qatar's world class service. They are the best airline because they pay attention to details that matter to the customer. The cabin crew is efficient professionals. I like that they always know what they are doing, as if they take pride in what they are doing. They are also very warm and personable.  They chat with you rather than talk to you - if you understand what I mean. I also like that they remember your preferences OR take initiative when they need to. My cabin attendant remembered that I preferred sparkling water and delivered it with each meal, but he also knew with which of my meal courses to serve my two different wine selections. The food is excellent and the restrooms are kept clean. Pilots y communicate ahead of time if there will be turbulence or if any information needs to be shared. Little things like these make me feel like they care and if they care then I feel comfortable and safe.

title: "Overall a great flight"
name: Michael Schade
review: Al Safwa lounge in Doha outstanding as always. Last minute aircraft switch from A350-1000 to B773, but was equipped with Qsuite. Holding area at Gate A7 did not provide seats for the fully booked flight. Flight departed 30 min later, which resulted in a 1h delay into Heathrow, because of the restricted airspace at the moment. Food was great. Caviar service was a nice touch. The service was ok, but lacked in consistency. Coffee was served 30 min before the meal arrived, and it also took up to 30 min to clear plate between courses during the meal. New WiFi lighting fast, which resulted in loud WiFi chats among some pax. I had to tell them to stop! I think this will become a big issue in the future and IFA should be firmer on those inconsiderate people. Overall a great flight with some room for improvement for onboard service

title: Service hectic and not overly friendly
name: Michael Schade

title: "herring bone business class"!
name: Alwaleed Althani
review: An ex Cathay Pacific 777 though this one was in a two class configuration probably from the ones used in the regional sectors of CX the herring bone business class was kept though the coloration and materials were Qatar Airways. The flight was full mid day departure from Doha with a strong tail wind arriving in Beirut a full 45 minutes early. IFE was non existent and WIFI worked well at the beginning but became non existent less than an hour into the flight. Catering was OK you can see that the route was not a priority.

title: "seats are terribly uncomfortable"
name: Debby van Zyl
route: Prague to Cape Town via Doha

title: "out and back were outstanding"
name: Maria Basanou
review: I had a flight multiple destinations trip booked both ways with qatar airways. From Athens - Doha and from Doha-Pnohm Penh via Saigon and the return from Bangkok-Doha-Athens. Both legs out and back were outstanding, Starting from the electronic check in and the well organised boarding and finishing with the baggage take out, the service in the plane and the food impeccable. The entertainment very updating and with new movies and games. Free wifi with excellent connectivity and the seats very comfortable for economy class. Also clean restrooms in the plane and the Doha airport super high class.
route: Athens to Phnom Penh via Doha

title: "Q-suites are really nice"
name: Jason Hill
review: We booked Sydney to Dublin return on business and later paid for an upgrade to first on the Doha - Sydney leg in both directions. The Q-suites in business are really nice and being able to close the door provides great privacy. The first class seats are even better with heaps of space but no ability to close off the area like with the Q-suites. Service in both business and first is excellent but it was a bit over the top in first and felt a bit insincere at times. The dedicated first class check-in at Doha is amazing with top notch personalised service and fast track security clearance. The Doha first class lounge is also amazing but on the return journey we were told that we shouldn't have had access to that lounge but they let us in. The big letdown on the flights was the food. We both had special vegan meals on all flights and the food was really bad. We found the Wi-Fi to be patchy during the flights. This may well have been the an issue with the third party provider but when you pay for unlimited Wi-Fi in business, it is frustrating when it doesn't work very well. This was our first time flying with Qatar and we had high hopes from an airline rated so highly. We've had better experiences with other airlines and we would be unlikely to fly Qatar again unless we specifically wanted to have another stopover in Doha.

title: "My food was poor"
name: A Kelsim
review: Very poor experience in January 2025. Check in was horrible with long wait. In-flight staff appeared exhausted. The plane was not connected to the airport and we had to be bused which was inconvenient. My food was poor. It was extremely disappointing.

title: "WIFI service is amazing"
name: Alwaleed Althani
review: Midday departure from Doha is a very quiet time as is evening arrival in LHR. We were in an older B777 and it was installed with Qsuites. The new WIFI service is amazing, and nice caviar and Balik salmon with Turbot rounded a delicious meal. The IFE game selection was poor

title: "comfortable and enjoyable flights"
name: Dhrubajyoti Samanta
review: I flew business class with Qatar Airways on their A350-900 featuring the Qsuite, and it turned out to be one of the most comfortable and enjoyable flights I’ve taken. Everything from the seat to the service felt thoughtfully designed and well-executed. It’s easy to see why Qatar Airways is so highly rated — this flight gave me a clear sense of what sets their business class apart. Qsuite privacy: the sliding door and fully enclosed suite created a personal sanctuary — perfect for work, rest, or relaxation. Full flat bed with soft pillows and a duvet for restful sleep. In-seat massage feature added to the comfort, especially on a long-haul journey. Gourmet-quality meals with a diverse menu. Every dish was beautifully presented and tasted amazing. Excellent selection of wines, spirits, and mocktails. High-quality amenity kit, soft pajamas, slippers, and other thoughtful touches. The cabin crew was warm, professional, and attentive throughout the journey. A350 cabin was quiet, smooth ride with mood lighting and large windows. Plenty of room to stow personal items. Wide selection of movies, TV shows, music, and more on a responsive touchscreen.

title: "service was personal and faultless"
name: Graham Henrickson
review: This was my first flight with Qatar and I arrived at the airport with anticipation.  was disappointed to learn I would not be travelling in their acclaimed Q Suite. They certainly lived up to what you would expect from an airline that has been awarded World’s Best Airline and recognised as having the World’s Best Business Class. There was one thing that needs to be addressed and that is the music played while boarding. It was ridiculously loud. Some of the things that really standout for me: Business Class passengers are given a sleeper suit to wear which was much appreciated. The inflight service was personal and faultless. The lay-flat function of the chair was extremely good, and the bedding material was great. The catering was the best I’ve experienced in a long time. The meals where truly exceptional and the fact they offer caviar to business class passengers is remarkable. This airline truly deserves the accolades it has received, and I can’t wait for my next flights with Qatar which at this stage will be in November this year.

title: "wonderful service provided"
name: Antony Peter
review: I was travelling to Cochin on Nov 21st 2024 going home for my vacation, and before i boarded the flight from Doha to Cochin i was upgraded from Economy to Business class in that sector. The service was exceptional and i still remember one of the flight attendant Aravind who was going extra mile to help me with the food. I am so proud to become a silver card member in Qatar Airways. Thank you Qatar Airways for all the wonderful service provided to passengers.

title: "The WIFI was super fast"
name: Alwaleed Althani
review: Excellent check in service at Male though it was high season in the Maldives and very high passenger volumes but they were efficient and courteous big shout out to Abdullah. The flight left ahead of time because of the great job the ground staff did. The plane had lie flat angled business class had a mid row but I did not mind as a night departure from Male is not like a day time departure which is a heavenly scene. Onboard service was nice and dinner was like Jeckel and Hyde the Tuna and Scallops appetizer was excellent but the steak overdone and unchewable. The WIFI was super fast I could view YouTube and everything else.

title: "best Business Class flight"
name: N. Siotto
review: Probably the best Business Class flight of my life and I have been traveling extensively in the last thirty years. Went from Dubai to Milan via Doha and back within five days. The configuration of the seats was different from one leg to another. The first leg was with the Q Suite game changer and the second with a more regular seat but still extremely comfortable. All flights punctual or landed earlier. In all routes the service was impeccable. The catering was comparable with a five star restaurant. Clean toilets. The comfort of the seats was unparalleled. Endless selection of movies. Onboard WiFi was very fast and reasonably priced.

title: "has become my favourite airline"
name: Don Beddage
review: Qatar Airways has become my favourite airline on the Toronto to Colombo route, which my family and I travel in Business Class, once or twice a year. I thoroughly enjoy the QSuite on the long leg of the flights to and from Doha. The privacy and the comfort offered by the QSuite is unparalleled to any other airline which operates on this route. The service from the cabin staff is outstanding, and I have enjoyed attentive service with a smile. Meal options and the option to enjoy meals at one’s convenience is an added feature. I appreciate the fact that, since last Autumn, Qatar flies in and out of Toronto Pearson Airport, which significantly reduces the travel time. We previously had to travel to Montreal in order to take Qatar Airways. Complimentary wifi on board is definitely appreciated.

title: "I complained on each flight"
name: Jonathan Griffiths
review: I flew from Tokyo Narita to Sofia via Doha and returned to Tokyo from Bucharest via Doha. Despite having reserved a vegetarian meal, on all flights crew forgot and I had to remind them. I complained on each flight and met the same reaction - a crew member telling me that this never usually happens, apologizing profusely, and promising that everything will be okay on the next flight. By the time it occurred for the 4th time on the 4th flight, I was finding it all rather frustrating and perplexing. On the flight from Bucharest to Doha I was given dirty cutlery and the cabin assistant whose attention I brought it to wasn't apologetic. She took the spoon from me and said it was a mistake and she would get a new one. In the end I had to call the senior cabin assistant who did apologize.  I found the inflight map system on 3 of the 4 flights to be extremely old fashioned. It was just a basic low-resolution non-interactive map of the kind that airlines used to provide when adding maps to the online entertainment was a new thing (fifteen or twenty years ago). On a long flight, I find the interactive maps to be very interesting and help to pass the time.  When I asked a crew member on the Bucharest to Doha flight how to check the movies I had placed on my favorites list, she said that such a feature does not exist on the inflight entertainment system for this aircraft. However, it did as I had already used it on a previous Qatar flight using the same model of aircraft and I had already added my favorite movies for this flight onto my list. I was unable to remember how to view the list. I tried to explain this to her, but she said that no such system existed and explained that not all aircraft have this feature. In the end, I played around for ten minutes or so and figured it out myself. I wouldn't have minded if the crew member was honest and said she didn't know or that she needed to check with someone else. I don't understand how Qatar are ranked up there with the top airline. The only thing that went well was that all four flights left on time.

title: still not heard from them for 5 weeks
name: Ian Goody
route: Perth to London Heathrow via Doha

title: could have just let me move"
name: T Reddy
verified: Trip | Since 2023, I have consistently avoided flying Qatar Airways because of my sub-par experience in Economy Class. There are only 2 options to Maputo either Qatar Airways or Ethiopian and I decided to go with Qatar. Mumbai to Qatar leg is operated by Indigo which has no IFE and on a 3.5 hour flight, you get a sandwich and a small juice with some crackers and really uncomfortable seats (I admit that was my mistake since I did not check thoroughly).  Boarding for the flight to Maputo was chaos by a disorganised gate agent. After departure they served a sandwich, due to the tight connection time, I did not have any time to pick up snacks at the airport. I ended up seated next to a plus sized lady with a rather fussy infant, who had a middle seat. Later, I saw there were quite a few empty seats and they could have just let me move and allowed both of us a bit of comfort, but by the time the seat belt signs were off, others had already spread out and fallen asleep in those seats. The main meal was served 1.5 hours before arrival in Maputo and while the taste was good it was inadequate. My IFE stopped working 5 hours or so into the flight.

title: Indigo service not up to expectations
name: T Reddy

title: "Very consistent service"
name: Luis De Jesus
review: Another great flight. Very consistent service, consistent quality of food and comfortable aircraft. The service on the flight was just spot on. I would recommend them based on how consistent they are.

title: "service was spot on"
name: Luis De Jesus
review: Amazing from beginning to end. Amazing service, food and cabin. The variety and the quality of the food is mind blowing. The service was spot on and attentive. The seat was comfortable and private.

title: "top quality crew"
name: Luis De Jesus
review: This flight was just the dream! 14hrs of first class service with top quality crew, amazing food, great Q Suite. The flight was so smooth, and pleasant, the hospitality was amazing.

title: "ruined a checked roller bag"
name: Mike Grandinetti
review: Dammam with a stop in Doha en route to Boston they ruined a checked roller bag of mine. 3 months later, I have yet to get any resolution. They have delayed repeatedly, sending me in an endless circle of unresponsive bureaucracy. I have lost respect for what was once my favorite airline.

title: "chaotic transfer at Hamad airport"
name: Michael Schade
review: Checkin, security and immigration at DXB airport swift, lounge with good a la carte options and friendly service. Boarding well organized. Delayed by 45 minutes due to bad weather. Flight uneventful. Despite delayed arrival, remote stand with bus transfer. It took me 50 minutes from arrival at remote stand till arrival at the terminal.  My plan to have a nice breakfast at the lounge was shattered. I had just a 10 minutes at the Platinum/Gold lounge, but left because there was no seat available, no clean cup to make a coffee, and long toilet queues. My flight to Bangkok was a remote stand bus gate. Boarding was poorly organized, as Business class and status guests were asked to wait till Economy has done boarding; the gate agent changed his mind and let C Class and status guests on the bus after 2 bus loads of Y class passengers had gone. It was rather chaotic. The flight on a leased CX aircraft was on time and uneventful. The IFE screens in the bulkhead rows of my section were broken, but large IPads were offered as a replacement, which was great. My food was mediocre. The flights were ok, but the chaotic transfer at Hamad airport left a bitter aftertaste.

title: "pleasant and uneventful"
name: Michael Schade
review: First leg was in Economy, 2nd in First. Check in at BKK airport fast and friendly. Contract lounge at the new Satellite terminal large, but limited western breakfast options. Boarding started 30 minutes late and another 15 minutes wait at the air bridge. Service was good with slightly improved F&B offerings. I was lucky to be on an aircraft with their upgraded WIFI services which worked real well. 3-4-3  configuration with rather hard seats. Transfer in Doha was good with a stay at the Al Safwa F lounge. Food was good. My final leg to Dubai was changed from a Qsuite to a 2-2-2 configuration. After many back and forth, they were willing to change to another Qsuite flight 30 minutes later. The flight was pleasant and uneventful.

title: "Qatar Airways changed the aircraft"
name: J Dayle
review: Qatar Airways changed the aircraft and did not notify us. We entered the Boeing 777 cabin to find that we had Paid for a Mercedes and would be flying in a Mini-Cooper. I didn't know airplanes with this drastically old configuration were still even in service. TIt was a miserable experience and we are changing our refundable return tickets to another airline. I won't be spending any money on Qatar Airways in the future!

title: "attendants were very helpful"
name: S Melkov
review: On my way to Doha, it was amazing, flight attendants were very helpful and attentive. Doha to Colombo was delayed and after take off asked for a drink which I was told would be served in about 10 minutes, it took more than an hour for cabin crew to star serving drinks and food. After the service they never came back to ask if anything else was needed.

title: "Both flights were excellent"
name: Walter Gujer
review: Both flights were excellent. Kind and professional staff on both flights, good food options (like that I can chose when to eat), comfortable flights. Due to distances at Doha airport, I appreciated that one of the small cart drivers offered his services to take me as close as possible to the train, connecting me to the center of the main terminal.

title: "discourtesy with the security staff"
name: K Brawn
review: I have been a frequent traveller throughout my nearly 60 years as an air passenger. In all that time, I have never encountered such an unprecedented level of discourtesy as I did with the security staff at the Qatar Airways gate. at Doha. My husband and I had already smoothly passed through two transit security checks with Qatar Airways on our return journey from New Zealand. As we tried to board Business Class, I was surprised to be pulled over again for another bag check. The treatment I received was rude and demeaning. Everything in my rucksack was unpacked, which was then swabbed inside and out. I was manually body searched in front of everybody and my clothes, shoes and even hair was swabbed. This took place for a prolonged period of time in front of every other passenger that filed past - the flight had been called. When I subsequently touched one of the items in the second tray of my belongings (to open the zip to demonstrate there were only documents inside) the lady raised her voice, ordering me to stand back and not to handle any of my possessions. I tried to explain I was only trying to help but was met with more incivility. I would add that my husband, who was stood with me, had to witness this affront without any recourse to offer his assistance. When I was finally allowed to leave - the lady having found nothing of any suspicion - I felt utterly humiliated. This awful experience with Qatar Airways was only saved by the helpful and impeccable service of a wonderfully professional air steward on board called Eugenia - thank you kind lady. Her humanity and warmth restored my equilibrium. However the overall experience of our return journey was that, with the exception of Eugenia, the customer service is appalling. . I understand security is important but there was no need for my encounter to be as intimidating, embarrassing and uncivil as it was.

title: "excited to fly again on an A380"
name: Walter Gujer
review: Very excited to fly again on an A380, my favorite aircraft. Check-in was nice and friendly, loved the lounge and staff there too! While the male crew were nice and looked happy, the female staff were mostly sour. Did not appreciate when the crew put my meal on my tray while I was at the bar. When I returned, the food was stone cold. I discussed that with the excellent purser. After that, the staff were sickly sweet. I decided to spend most of the time at the bar. Shame, but the next flight on Qatar was again excellent.

title: customer services unhelpful
name: Tilly Hoskins
route: London Heathrow to Hong Kong via Doha

title: cabin crew worked tirelessly
name: Tai Wei Wong
route: Kuala Lumpur to Houston via Doha

title: "the service is always so good"
name: Claus Falsig
review: I fly often with Qatar. Over 10 times per year, and the service is always so good. Very nice staff both at ground and at Cabin. I usually fly Economy and rarely get upgraded or I more often use points to upgrade my self. But still on long flights from Europe to Asia over Doha on Economy is a great experience.

title: "consistently ranks at or near the top"
name: Rajan Parrikar
review: Doha to Goa (Mopa Airport) in Business Class on Qatar Airways. This short flight, lasting just under three hours, featured Qatar Airways' non-Qsuite Business Class seats. Despite the absence of Qsuite, the experience was supremely comfortable, as is expected. The onboard service was warm and attentive, complemented by their signature on-demand meal offerings. The seamless blend of comfort, service, and sophistication once again highlights why Qatar Airways’ Business Class consistently ranks at or near the top.

title: "noticeable upgrade in meal quality"
name: Rajan Parrikar
review: Copenhagen to Doha on Qatar Airways in Business Class in Dreamliner 787-9. This aircraft features Qatar Airways’ standard non-Qsuite Business Class seating configuration. Despite the absence of the Qsuite, the seats are exceptionally comfortable, ensuring a luxurious experience. The inflight service was flawless, showcasing their hallmark on-demand meal service. I was particularly impressed by the noticeable upgrade in meal quality. The vegetarian option was thoughtfully prepared, with a refreshing lightness compared to the heavier, oilier meals of previous years. Overall, this was an outstandingly comfortable flight that lives up to Qatar Airways’ reputation for delivering the finest Business Class experience in the skies.

title: "the flight with Qatar Airways was great"
name: L Han
review: Overall the flight with Qatar Airways itself was great, but the flight with one of its partner Malaysia Airlines to KUL is just terrible. For the Munich to Doha route, usually it is flown by the Boeing 787 or A350-900 which does not have QSuite, but on the day of my travel, we got the 777 with QSuite which was a huge upgrade from these types of planes that usually operate this route. Food was great, Staff was great, Entertainment was Good, WIFI is free for Privilege Club Member up to 1 hour. Arrived in Doha and we enjoy their Al Mourjan Business Lounge located at The Garden. Then we continue to Kuala Lumpur with Malaysia Airlines and the food was terrible, entertainment was very limited, WIFI was slow and staff was not so great. Qatar Airways needs to stop handing over their flights to their partners like MH and continue to operate their own daily flights instead.

title: "pretty disappointing flight"
name: Hubert Josef Lefarth
review: We had a pretty disappointing flight. As we boarded in Barcelona, ​​I noticed that the flight attendants didn't seem particularly friendly. The plane was old, my partner's screen didn't work, and the seat in front of me was difficult to recline. Service on the night flight didn't start until 1.5 hours after takeoff, which pretty much ruined our night. The quality of the food has been better before, there were no refills of drinks and no coffee before landing let alone breakfast. We are very disappointed this time and hope that it was just a bad exception and that Qatar will maintain its level in 2025.

title: "the service was impeccable"
name: E Besine
review: flew with Qatar Airways for a round trip between Paris CDG and Manila, with a layover in Doha. The overall experience was outstanding, both on the outbound and return journeys. The layovers were perfectly coordinated: around 2 hours on the outbound flight and less than an hour on the return. Despite their short duration, everything was handled smoothly and efficiently, without any stress. Onboard, the service was impeccable, with an attentive and professional crew. The food served was of high quality, and the seats were exceptionally comfortable, even on a long-haul flight. I highly recommend Qatar Airways for its seamless organization, exceptional service, and commitment to passenger comfort
route: Paris CDG to Manila via Doha

title: cabin crew are warm and friendly
name: Theona Knight

title: nothing arranged for the missed flight
name: T Nieminen

title: "I was not recognized in any way"
name: Philippe Gimenez
review: As an emerald platinum member of Finnair Oneworld alliance, I was not recognized in any way: no welcome drink, no words of welcome.  On the HKG-Doha flight, which took 9 hours 30 minutes, I was served was a disgusting snack and a mediocre breakfast with coffee that tasted burnt. The cabin temperature was terribly cold and nothing was done to change things. The icing on the cake was the distant, even contemptuous attitude of the flight attendants to me.
route: Hong Kong to Paris CDG via Doha

title: "Exceptional service"
name: Lucette Nyberg
review: Exceptional service, always kind and did their best to make you feel comfortable. Did not have a q suite and the seats may need be refreshed but did not matter when you get nice service good food drinks . I never felt discriminated as i often do because i am overweight, it may sound stupid but for me who travels a bit, that feeling is horrible. Thanks Qatar. Keep up the good job!!!

title: "superb experience"
name: M Darsen
review: As always superb experience! Perfect service, friendly and attentive cabin crew, clean and fresh cabin. Food excellent!

title: "Perfect service"
name: M Darsen
review: As always superb experience! Perfect service, friendly and attentive cabin crew, clean and fresh cabin. Food excellent! And took me back to Air France in the 90s on long-haul flights, a lounge in the business class cabin. Excellent for stretching your legs, get a drink and mingle with other passengers. Splendid idea! But not free basic WiFi.

title: "downgraded my plane"
name: Kevin Kozinchik
review: I have flown them 6 times in the last year from Madrid to South Africa via Doha and in business class. Those who have flown on their Q-Suites (777), 787s, A350s, and their older 777 where it was 2 seats on the window with no real divider or privacy, understand there is a huge difference in quality between their planes and the experience overall. When choosing my route for an upcoming trip with them I chose a route that had a Q-suite followed by a 787, as they offer the most privacy and comfort. I just received notice that they have downgraded my plane from Doha to Johannesburg to their oldest configuration of their 777 where there are 2 seats on the window, no real divider, and no privacy at all. I understand that sometimes changes are necessary, however I've spent 3 days now trying to get them to change the 1 leg of my journey, the leg where they downgraded the plane to the old 777, to an alternative flight that would simply put me back on the 787. I'm not looking for an upgrade to a Q-suite, I just don't want to be on the same plane I booked and paid for. However, their service team refuses to make the change without me paying a fare difference. Apparently the only time they will allow a free change is if they downgrade you from the Q-suite, which is ridiculous. If they moved it from a 787 to an A350 it would have been fine, however their old 777 configuration is by no means anywhere near the same quality of experience. There is no flex, no desire to keep your business. I realize I am complaining about a business class experience, which may seem petty to some, however when you fly a lot, spend a lot of money to do so, there should be an expectation of service from the partners you choose.

title: "serious confusion"
name: D Mayle
review: There was serious confusion conveyed by flight attendants to passengers after arriving in Phuket. “Passengers flying on to Penang should disembark at Phuket.” “Passengers going on to Penang should stay seated.” “Passengers who will go on to Penang should come forward now to disembark.” “You should not be trying to disembark yet.” Now you need to disembark at the rear of the plane.” “You should not be coming back here. Return to the front.” Then a scheduled 90 minute layover became 3 hours. Upon arrival in Penang Qatar did not get a jetway, and it was raining hard. After moving forward to disembark we were told by the pilot that the airport is requiring us to sit on the aircraft for 30 minutes until the rain stops. About 40 minutes later the pilot informed us that we will have to wait at least another 10 minutes.

title: "too bland or salty"
name: Harish Vasudevan
review: The food and choices on the long haul from Bos-Doh was quite stressful. The Economy regular meals were to die for to be honest- some of the best chicken biryani, which you wouldn’t see in the special meal menus. This time it was stuff that neither me nor my family could get half way through. Either too bland or salty. Also the service was slow - kids get super cranky on late night flights without food and no butter, salt, pepper and sugar.  Unlike many other airlines, QR’s economy class is really good. In fact that was my biggest selling point for wanting to fly QR this time- collecting air miles is not an easy job! An older aircraft having >90% occupancy with an out of order toilet plus wi-fi doesn’t help. On the positive side, massive shout out to QR ground staff from Bos for a smooth path from check in to boarding. Also to the Doh- MAA flight pursers for explaining the shortage of on board meal options- only chicken was available in the general category.

title: no feedback in a month
name: R Keale
review: It started when we checked in at Maldives and the check-in couldn't give us any boarding passes for the second flight between Doha and Copenhagen. They asked us to contact the Qatar Airways service desk after arriving in Doha. We did this upon arrival but was then referred to another Qatar Airways service desk. After queuing our way through the third service desk, we were told that they could not give us a boarding pass due to the flight being overbooked. They wanted us to go to some other service desk several times. After 3 hours of waiting we finally got boarding shortly after check-in at the gate opened. We had to rush to the gate. Since I had not received a boarding pass at the airport, I could not do any purchases from the airport shops as I normally do. While I wasn't checked in, all the updates via Qatar Airways' app on my mobile didn't show up, which features I appreciate. I have sent an email to their customer service support but no feedback in a month.

title: "comfortable 5 hours in the air"
name: Virginia Bolton
review: Qatar Airways is using Oman Air planes and crew on certain sectors. This sector was changed a few months after I booked my ticket, and I didn’t mind so much because I was keen to experience Oman Air. The business class layout is ok, and the seat was very comfortable. There were only 3 passengers travelling in business class on this flight and we were well attended to. The food and wine was good. Nothing more, nothing less. The crew were very pleasant and enjoyed a chat. I find the Qatar entertainment very limited and not a wide selection of movies. The flight itself was very smooth. Overall, a comfortable 5 hours in the air.

title: "Outstanding flight and crew"
name: Virginia Bolton
review: Outstanding flight and crew. The food and wine were superb. The FA looking after our section was outstanding in her quiet delivery of her service. I had a very different experience on each of the four sectors I flew, and this sector together with the Doha/Nairobi sector were on par. Attentive service, good food and a comfy seat. The entertainment choices are very limited so I usually end up listening to my own music. The seat was very comfortable and I managed to get 6 hours sleep.

title: "flight lived up to expectations"
name: G Leyne
review: The flight lived up to expectations, though this older aircraft did not have as wide a range of movies loaded into its IFE compared to the flights operated by newer A350 and 787. Boarding of the aircraft was efficient and respected the assigned seating Zones. Good service from cabin crew, who were very courteous. The first meal service was a bland chicken sandwich (because the flight operated late at night, when most people just wanted to sleep). A full cooked meal service was provided later in the flight for breakfast, however. The flight operated on time.

title: "Onboard service was excellent"
name: S Deylan
review: The best thing about this airline is the good training of its staff. They have been trained to be polite even in the face of problems. It goes a long way to making the experience more pleasant than it otherwise could have been. The booking experience  via their website was not fully satisfactory as the seat map could not be accessed at the time of booking nor via 'Manage your booking' after the booking was made. I had to ring up the call centre  to request them to book my specific seats. The call-centre staff were polite and very willing to help, but the quality of the phone line was poor. Online check-in could not be fully completed, requiring manual document checks at the airport (Heathrow) for the issue of the boarding pass. However, the queues for the self-service check-in machines at the airport was very long and every passenger seemed to require manual documentation check by staff (who were too few to cope with this - but handled it politely)s. Onboard service was excellent and plenty of food provided for the flight. IFE had an impressive selection of movies and TV shows.

title: "leave the queue to adjust the weight"
name: V Hargan
review: Traveling from Delhi to Düsseldorf, we encountered a long check-in queue, some open counters in the economy class were left unattended, with staff chatting instead of assisting passengers. We had an allowed baggage weight of 100 kg for our four tickets, each with a 25 kg limit. We checked in two bags, but the staff said that one bag weighed 27 kg instead of 25 kg and demanded payment for the extra 2 kg. We had to leave the queue to adjust the weight and upon returning, were asked to start from the end of the line. Onboard I had to settle for an Indian vegetarian meal, which I did not enjoy. While Doha airport was beautiful, we were transported to our connecting flight to Düsseldorf by bus, which was uncomfortable.

title: "my food did not taste good"
name: S Gale
review: The staff were not friendly to me at check in. The seats aren't comfortable and my food did not taste good. Planes are smaller than some airlines.

title: "They were so kind and helpful"
name: M Hanoje
review: I travelled with a baby on Qatar Airways. Though I added the baby late, they auto-assigned me a seat with a bassinet. On the flight, they were so kind and helpful. They had baby food and diapers. They helped me fetch milk from my bag. When the baby poured food all over me, they helped me clean up, including a member who wiped my feet. They were so kind and helpful - I was very moved.

title: only offer you what is left"
name: M Lopez
route: Ho Chi Minh City to Barcelona via Doha

title: "meal service was far below expectations"
name: M Saunders
route: New York to Mumbai via Doha

title: cut my trip short by one day"
name: Harley Salinas

title: "serve me cold omelette"
name: S Lobarda
review: I always fly business class with Qatar Airways and this is the first time I got disappointed with the service, especially with the food. They serve me cold omelette and I asked for cappuccino they serve me like a glass of milk with a small amount of coffee.

title: "expect them to provide transit accommodation"
name: Ameer Ali
review: I booked business class KTM-DOH-LHR and back with British Airways and Qatar Airways. 2 days before my flight back from London I get a message stating they have cancelled my connecting flight from Doha to KTM and replaced it with a flight next morning, with a 10 hour layover. I expect them to do is provide transit accommodation in Doha, when they they chose to put me through the unwanted 10 hour layover but they don't. I am asked to land in Doha and try my luck with the transfer desk, I am not amused by the thought of running around for accommodation past midnight when my flight lands. I call them, chat with them, my agent in KTM files a request , my travel agent sends a mail, and I filed a formal complaint. Nothing.

title: cancel the flight for operational reasons"
name: J Gregory

title: did not receive a premium experience
name: Mark Curran

title: Wifi on both legs was terrible
name: Mark Curran
route: Dublin to Cape Town via Doha

title: service outstanding from start to finish
name: Mouaad Joumani

title: "I wholeheartedly recommend them"
name: H Khaldin
review: I've had the pleasure of flying with various airlines over the years, but Qatar Airways remains my top choice. Their exceptional service consistently impresses me, from lounge amenities to seamless departures and in-flight experiences, culminating in stress-free arrivals. Qatar Airways has earned my loyalty, and I wholeheartedly recommend them to friends and colleagues.

title: "a very nice Economy experience"
name: J Hayne
review: Evening departure from BKK. Qatar really struggle with boarding on occasions and this was no different. Seats were changed at the gate as one of our reserved one’s were wet. Allocated into the rear cabin 38A + B. Seemed to be an older aircraft with an older configuration in the business cabin. Busy flight. The seat proved to be very comfortable and superbly thought out. The best there is I think. Safety issue of individuals roaming around the cabin aisles looking for empty seat rows, including someone moving up and down the aisle with his bags, opening and shutting overhead bins blocking the aisle While the aircraft is taxiing under power for departure. Something that should of been jumped on very quickly, but wasn’t. Food was exceptional for Economy, as was the service from a hard working pleasant crew. Overall this sector proved that this is the best Medium to long haul Economy cabin product there is. . Doha Hamad Airport is exceptional and a pleasant transfer. Second Sector on a Boeing 787-8. 19F + G either side of an aisle. Flight appeared to be full and Departed on time, early hours of the morning. The seat’s on this Aircraft were significantly less comfortable. A . 3-3-3 configuration and the ACDFGJ seats each have an under seat IFE box mounted on it’s side that restricts the width of legroom available. Seats appear far narrower, and not so well thought out. Although I find the cabin appealing and the ride pleasant. I’ll generally avoid this particular type of Aircraft. because I find them particularly uncomfortable in Economy, especially on a busy flight.. Again, exceptional food, great Service and a lovely hard working crew. Nonetheless, overall a very nice Economy experience, highly recommend it.

title: "sets a high standard"
name: Rashid Alammari
review: As a frequent flyer with Qatar Airways, I can confidently say that their service is consistently exceptional. The hospitality, from check-in to arrival, is outstanding; staff are courteous, attentive, and always ready to assist. Their scheduling is reliable and well-organized, making flights punctual and connections smooth. The ease of booking, online or through the app is incredibly user-friendly, allowing me to arrange trips without hassle. The lounge facilities are superb, offering a quiet, luxurious space to relax before flights with excellent dining options. Both ground and inflight services are top-notch, with a focus on comfort, quality, and efficiency. Qatar Airways truly sets a high standard in global air travel!

title: "rudely pushed upright by passengers"
name: A Mears
review: Doha to Athens plane was small with box that blocked leg room window seats. As a person with a disability I could not move, request to move was met with only had middle seat, as business class is only for people that pay for them. When I requested to pay by points for upgrade was told none available. When people in front chose to recline hostess reaction they are allowed to recline however when we tried our seats were rudely pushed upright by passengers. I have emailed Qatar as yet no response.

title: "like being in a 5 star hotel"
name: Martin Halshaw
review: In the past 10 days or so I've taken 4 long haul business class flights with Qatar and on all of them it's been like being in a 5 star hotel / restaurant. The cabin crew treated me like a VIP, the food & drinks were excellent and the facilities (seat, entertainment, wifi, toilets etc) were all fantastic. It is a real pleasure flying QA. The same goes for the lounges I used in Doha with the only negative being the lounges which they don't control outside of Doha.

title: Breakfast portions dismal
name: Brian Davies
review: Absolutely terrible trip. Started off well from Dubai to Doha nice 777 in first class which is business class rebranded for this route. Once in Doha the fun started in the Business Class lounge I was told I could only stay 30 minutes and access to the restaurant area was denied because they were busy, so no hot food.
