Staff were robotic and not at all interested. The connecting flight was an old A320 with a business class seat which was broken and they said cannot be fixed. Hardly any legroom and cramped. Poor inflight entertainment selection and no wifi. Breakfast portions dismal, left the flight hungry.

title: "luggage had been severely damaged"
name: R Han
review: I discovered that my luggage had been severely damaged during the journey. The crack spanned across the depth of the luggage, from the zipper to the wheel area, and the inner linings of my baggage were clearly visible. As I had left the Airport premises when the damage was found and I acknowledged that there might not be anything the airline could have done for me, their response was the most unhelpful I have ever received. My return flight on 29 September was cancelled. The instructions to collect our baggage and hotel vouchers were poorly communicated, we only heard muffled announcements and did not receive any email or text message on what to do next. It was chaotic and the airline staff were unhelpful, it took us more than 4 hours just to collect our baggage and vouchers at the airport, before we boarded the bus to the assigned hotel.  Following my trip, I also reached out to Qatar Airways Media/Customer Service to share my feedback, however, it does not seem like they took my feedback seriously. They took more than 10 days to inform me that they had forwarded my feedback on baggage irregularities to the Baggage department in Zurich.

title: " they really are customer focused"
name: Craig Cutts
review: I have flown with QR 8 times this month, economy and business class. The airline is pretty good especially in economy as a Privilege club member with status, expedited check in and lounge access for both Silver & Gold levels. On board assuming you pick the right seat it's comfortable The food is good as is the service, it's very good and probably one of the best. Business class is great on any aircraft, I had my first experience of Q Suites which was nice, although I like the A380 too with a bit more space and a lounge to use. My 8th flight this month in Q suites from DOH to LHR was probably the least enjoyable, boarding was a shambles, the aircraft was a 20 minute bus ride away and once on board remained at 30c + for 45 minutes, the crew were just okay. Food options not available and the area around my seat is not cleaned with rubbish left by the previous passenger. Clearly QR and DOH airport offer great options, we all have off days so hopefully this is what happened with my last flight. Otherwise I will continue to use QR on my 8 to 10 trips each year, they really are customer focused and offer a great product they just need to get there boarding process sorted it's a bit of a let down sometimes.

title: "complaints are being overlooked"
name: Michael van de Tonnekreek
review: Rude Staff Upon arrival at the airport, we encountered dismissive behavior from a Qatar Airways supervisor, our concerns were met with a lack of empathy, which  No Entertainment during our 7-hour flight, my wife and I were the only passengers without a functioning entertainment system. While I understand that technical issues can occur, there was no effort to provide alternatives. This left us frustrated and disappointed. I have reached out to Qatar Airways multiple times regarding these issues, but their responses have been generic and focused on unrelated matters, such as visa documentation. It feels as if our legitimate complaints are being overlooked. We chose Qatar Airways expecting world-class service, but we received anything but that. The lack of resolution has overshadowed what should have been a joyful occasion.

title: "pleased with the overall comfort and service"
name: A Ahmed
review: No hot towels are provided, and the sweet to suck to prevent your ears-popping are no longer given out and meals were notably smaller although still sufficient. We did online check-in and there was dedicated web bag-drop which was quick, and stress-free where the friendly ground staff issued us paper boarding passes. Boarding was scheduled early but on time, and the cabin crew on our first flight from London Gatwick to Doha seemed motivated, hospitable and keen to please providing service with a warm smile. The Boeing 787 on this route is very comfortable to travel on and we arrived early into Doha, where thankfully we could have a leisurely transfer via an airbridge to the terminal and even have some time to experience The Orchard at Doha Hamad as well as a quick spot of Duty Free shopping. Our second flight to Salalah was on time, staff were a little less polished but efficient and friendly. Meals including snacks on both sectors was excellent in quality - very tasty. I was pleased with the overall comfort and service, and it provided a relaxing start to our holiday in Oman.

title: "My food was appalling"
name: A Ahmed
review: Our outbound journey with Qatar Airways excellent and I was pleased standard had been maintained but our return journey showed how inconsistent they cab ne and that an excellent experience cannot always be counted on. After checking in online where it was communicated our flight from Doha to London would be on an A380 which we were pleased about, when we went to the airport to bag-drop we enquired if we could be seated on the upper deck economy cabin - the check-in assistant at Salalah airport was struggling to do this so consulted the Qatar Airways station manager there and he informed us the reason it wasn't possible was the plane had been changed to a 777. This plane is is cramped with 10 abreast rows when the plane is designed for 9 abreast, the seats have virtually no padding. On an overnight flight it was a very uncomfortable. The transfer experience early morning at Doha to get to that plane is stressful and chaotic - especially if they make you land at a remote stand, you will struggle to make your connecting flight. It is ridiculous to be using a remote stands and bussing people to the terminal via slow convoluted route at what is very new and modern airport. There is also bag security checks, once a general one then another random one at the gate for our flight back to London. My food was appalling on all sectors with this journey - the funny thing is on our Salalah to Doha sector we got the same meal (chicken mashkool) as on our way to Salalah a week ago, but whilst then it tasted amazing this time it tasted terrible. Cabin crew on both journeys were at best indifferent, but looked unhelpful, unmotivated, lacking warmth and miserable on the second flight from Doha to London Heathrow - this is meant to be one of their flagship routes, yet this is the standard of service.

title: "My food was disgusting"
name: P Talpahti
review: Helsinki-Doha operated by Finnair. Excellent service and food very good for economy class, FAs almost perfect. Qatar Airways from Doha to Johannesburg. This flight was the biggest disappointment. Service was we don't care, we just work here. My food was disgusting. Drinks served in glasses.

title: "no cordoned off section for Business Class"
name: E Keele
review: On 7 October 2024 we had waited for hours in Barcelona airport and were looking forward to relax in a pleasant Business Class lounge. Our dep 2250 for Doha flight checkin time was stated to be 7.45 pm, but the staff were busy chatting, not heeding the time and long queue for Economy. There was no cordoned off section for Business Class passengers. They finally opened 3 counters past check in time and it was a free for all with Economy class passengers shoving ahead. The Barcelona business class lounge, it was very disappointing. Most toilets did not work, there was hair and rubbish stuck in the sinks. In the lounge none of the power points worked for us to charge up our phones. The food offered was the worst in a Business Class Lounge. A few squares of cold lasagne, lots of snacks, and cleaning staff wheeled their wagons around the tables chucking in dirty tableware noisily. The flight to Barcelona to Doha took off late. As a result, in Doha we were not able to enjoy the Business Class lounge, and barely managed to get to our connecting flight to Kuala Lumpur, rushing through the crowded airport.
route: Barcelona to Kuala Lumpur via Doha

title: "no compensation will be paid"
name: D Norton
review: The flight from Rome to Doha was turned back and had to land in Turkey where the passengers stayed on board the plane for more than five hours before being sent to sit in seats at a departure gate. Without clear advice on the flight out of Turkey we eventually were placed on a flight to Doha. There was chaos at Doha airport with little help and communication in regard to flights to get home to Perth. The systems seemed inadequate to provide information to staff who were extremely inefficient in handling our flights back to Perth. After a further period of about 10 hours we were advised last minute of flights we were on. We had paid extra for the upper deck of an A380 plane from Doha to Perth and were placed in normal economy on the flight to Singapore. We were then placed on a Jetstar budget airline flight from Singapore to Perth.  We arrived at our destination two days late after experiencing about 4 hours of sleep over the two day period. After seeking compensation for the Doha to Perth flight, particularly for the upper deck seats we never got, they advised me that no compensation will be paid.

title: happy with the way this trip was handled
name: Ladislav Habart

title: "air crew were particularly helpful"
name: Reneeta DSilva
review: Amazed by the service on ground, in the air, quick baggage delivery, on time flights, clean aircraft, toilets, super entertainment. The air crew were particularly helpful and went out of their way to help, respectful and very professional. First time flew Qatar Airways but will keep an eye out when selecting airline in future. Keep it up!!

title: "only get an aisle seat at the airport"
name: Svetlana Ceban

title: "app is an utterly appalling user experience"
name: C Harland
review: Return trip LHR via Doha to Kuala Lumpur via, economy. Check-in via laptop failed because log-in all day said "There's been an error. Please try later". The app failed to work because required fields (Nationality, and Country Code - already in my profile details, but not propagated to my booking) were inert and couldn't be completed - and so I couldn't proceed to the next page. Eventually, via laptop I got a Confirmation of Check-in, but no boarding passes - which meant queueing and check in at Heathrow anyway on the day of departure. A 1-hour connection time in Doha was reduced to 30 minutes due to late arrival of our first Qatar flight, and we had to cover a long distance to the next gate (including wife with 6-month old knee replacement). It was tense, and if they hadn't delayed the subsequent flight by 20 minutes we would never have made it. Return check-in the app didn't work. We had therefore to check in at the airport in Kuala Lumpur on the morning of the flight - along with dozens and dozens of other passengers presumably with the same issue.  There was a massive queue as a result. It moved at a snail's pace because even with five desks open, the process and staff seemed laborious and slow. We barely made the gate in time, but had allowed two and a half hours for this. During the second return leg (Doha - London) the crew were pretty clumsy, one girl ramming the drinks trolley three times into my seat and the seat in front. I'm not oversized, but got barged by cabin crew going past twice, which is profoundly annoying when you're trying to sleep. The crew on this leg were not particularly friendly, although on other legs they were pleasant. The app is very good at suggesting you upgrade your seat or baggage (at cost) but is so short of real-time data as to be useless. With a paper boarding pass in hand, two hours before wheels up in Doha for LHR, the app still didn't even know I had a second flight to London. The app is an utterly appalling user experience. All the above made for far too tense an experience in both directions, so I won't be using Qatar Airways again. I chose them on the basis of good reviews, and really wanted to like Qatar Airways, but I'm disillusioned. Fairly good in-flight food and good entertainment options are not enough to mitigate all the other issues, which tarnished the start and end of a holiday.
route: London to Kuala Lumpur via Doha

title: "flight experience was splendid"
name: Farooq Shah
review: The overall flight experience was splendid, stay at Hammad International Airport felt amazing (9 hours layover) without an inconvenience. The flight from Riyadh to Doha was a bit delayed but the connecting flights were properly managed as well as the Pilot notified this when we boarded the plane. Everything was pre-announced to ensure safety of passengers including turbulence indication. Loved how the crew was extremely professional assisting throughout the flight from Doha to Male.

title: "I find the legroom lacking"
name: Amy Cooper
review: I've flown Qatar now a few times and each time previously I wasn't sure what to write about the airline. I usually fly Emirates from GLA and even though Emirates service  has deteriorated in recent years I maintain that they are better.  The first 2 sectors from edi to KUL was so uncomfortable. I am only 5 ft and I find the legroom lacking, the width of seat was just fine for me but unfortunately the guy in the middle spilled onto mine and the one on the other side too. Online check-in was unavailable despite being told one can do everything on their app. Customer support via chat was next to useless and telephone calls went unanswered.
route: Edinburgh to Kuala Lumpur via Doha

title: "requested cash compensation"
name: J Meyre
route: Nairobi to San Francisco via Doha

title: "Food was in a small portion"
name: Md Daud Ismail
review: The aircraft was in good condition. Boarding were by zone which was strictly followed and very efficient. Food was in a small portion and limited option for Southeast Asians. The cabin crew was doing their job but not the best that I have experienced. The serving of food and beverages was slow on return flights. One young female cabin crew from Indonesia interacted with customers (KUL-DOH sector) and was very hard working.

title: "food was in a small portion"
name: Md Daud Ismail
review: The aircraft was old and the toilet door was noisy during take-off and landing. The seat back couldn't move and was very uncomfortable despite sitting in the preferred seat at the emergency exit. The food was in a small portion and the serving was slow. The cabin crew was okay but not efficient. I paid for the high-fare class for an economy seat and was not impressed with the experience. Will fly this airline if the price is much lower.

title: "1 meal service on a 7 hour flight"
name: Jan Colson
review: I flew QR a number of times before the pandemic and was looking forward to flying then again. With an airline with a reputation like Qatar Airways, I expect an above average experience compared to other full-service airlines. To my surprise, this time was well below average, both outbound and inbound. Only 1 meal service on a 7 hour flight.  The quality of my food was really poor. I ordered the chicken dish and what I received was mashed potatoes with a bland sauce and two minuscule pieces of chicken.  Staff was friendly and helpful. So good points there. My impression is that Qatar Airways has been focusing on reducing costs.

title: "Food poor in quality"
name: Michael Schade
review: Check in at Bangkok airport efficient and friendly. New Miracle contract lounge at new Bangkok terminal better than those at the old terminal. Boarding well organized. Seats of the aging B777 are poorly padded and have below average seat pitch. Service provided by a flight attendant who really does not enjoy her job. Food poor in quality, taste and presentation. Flight on time. Champagne at the Platinum South lounge at Doha airport has been replaced by not very nice sparkling wine. Departure at Doha airport for Dubai from the feared downstairs Bus gates. It had the atmosphere of a budget terminal with insufficient seating, noise and unorganized boarding. It was off peak time at Doha airport with plenty of airbridges available! Long wait on the bus with open doors at 40 degrees. Short flight to Dubai on time on a wonderful and comfortable A350-1000.

title: "One of the best airlines"
name: P Dassene
review: One of the best airlines I ever flew with. The quality of the service and the plane is over the top, and they still managed to be less expensive that other companies! I love them.

title: "cabin staff were fantastic"
name: Alwaleed Althani
review: Probably one of the first A380s received by Qatar Airways and the age showed. That said the cabin staff were fantastic expressive and attentive, the IFE was superb and the selection was wide though I would suggest a search option. WI-FI wasn’t working for me Qatar Airways need to refurbish their A380s and not wait on Boeing the 777-9s that are a long way off.

title: a pleasure to be on board
name: Anne Jenner

title: help us find our missing luggages
name: L Alami

title: "waiting for a response to my complaint"
name: P Darson
review: One of the people in the group is 6 feet 4 inches so I booked and paid for three front row seats on the Qatar Airways web site. The payment for just over £240 went through and was confirmed. When I checked in we had been moved to a normal economy seat. The check-in staff at Dubai (not Qatar Airways staff) tried to resolve it but couldn't. I was told to go to the transfer desk at Doha where they would have an answer. The staff there could not care less. The answer was 'there is a family sat there so there is nothing we can do'. Then seemed surprised that I wanted a refund and handed me a card at me with some email addresses on it. We boarded and the seats we should have had were occupied by three other people. No babies, no baby bassinets or any other reason we were moved out of them. The tall person in the group had to sit with one leg in the aisle and one under the middle seat. It was uncomfortable. The seat pitch on the 787 was tight and of course the person in front reclined as soon as the aircraft left the ground. My food was rubbish. I did get a refund but 2 weeks later I am still waiting for a response to my complaint. I am unsure how Qatar presents itself as a 5 star airline.

title: "Customer service at Doha was very poor"
name: Astrid Toegimin

title: "delayed for 8 hours"
name: Jay Shah
route: San Francisco to Ahmedabad via Doha

title: "Really poor experience"
name: C Marwan
review: Really poor experience. After a missed connection I was offered an overnight hotel stay . I really didn’t want this so asked to stay overnight in the airport and go on an earlier flight. By 2:30am my only solution was to accept the hotel which was a 30 min bus ride from the airport. I travelled on a very shoddy mini bus with 10 men (I was the only female) at 2:30am to a very old and run down hotel. Checking in took another 30 mins where I refused to sign a document accepting I felt safe in the hotel. I was left with no other option . When I raised a complaint with Qatar once I finally got home they do not care. Not worth it especially if it’s a tight connection in Doha. Customer service on the ground had an attitude and we’re not listening to my concerns.

title: "I can't even say how grateful I am"
name: Armine Hovsepyan

title: "overall it was a good flight"
name: Emilio Chrisostomou
review: The check in process was smooth and simple, they had self check in too which I liked, within 45 minutes I was sitting at the gate waiting to board my 10.45pm flight to Doha. When I arrived on the aircraft I was very kindly greeted by the cabin crew, it was lovely and bright when I got into the aircraft. When I arrived at my seat there was a pillow, blanket, headphones, and amenity kit waiting for me, they also had an adjustable head rest and reading light. Very good variety of entertainment on there and I was very impressed with how attentive the touchscreen was. About 1 hour after take-off they served the meal, it was very good food, then for about 7 hours they let you rest and then another meal - for me it was dinner and breakfast given the time of the flight. Qatar Airways food is not too bad at all! It was a smooth take-off and landing, overall it was a good flight, better than I expected and can't wait to fly home from holiday with Qatar Airways.

title: "worst possible check in Singapore"
name: Ranjan Ramchandani
review: The worst possible check in Singapore. We were a family of three flying to Washington DC on 28th May in business class. The check in staff was uninterested, unhelpful almost to the point of being rude. She was taking almost 10 mins per passenger at check in. Onboard service was fantastic and second to none; the true Qatar service I had seen on a previous flight I had taken, but after this incident at Singapore airport, I would never fly Qatar Airways out of Singapore. It’s too stressful.

title: "Qatar Airways is my first option"
name: Herman Troubleyn
review: It was a mixed class. Flying economy with my favourite plane the A380. And Business from Doha to Brussels. We were able to get an upgrade for the second flight. By filling up the remaining seats Qatar is letting you taste of their excellent service in business. It's quite addictive. We got seats 1E and 1F and could enjoy the great food and during a sanitary stop my seat was converted to a bed and we could get a really good sleep during the flight. On the first flight from Bangkok to Doha we were seated in the A380 which is very quiet and the seats are large enough to fit my large legs. I've contacted the helpdesk (by chat/ telephone) numerous times and they react quickly and very friendly. For me Qatar Airways is my first option to look for a next flight.

title: "Had a very good flight"
name: Alimah Lawrie
review: Had a very good flight with Qatar Airways. The cabin manager, Khalid, was superb in his service. He gave true and excellent service throughout the flight. Overall smooth flight from Singapore to Doha, had my 10 year old daughter with me. Only setback ,no ice cream on board.

title: always a pleasure to fly with Qatar
name: Erich Lichtscheidl
route: Hong Kong to Vienna via Doha

title: assume I was not served the right meal
name: L Goh

title: "not able to provide the same food"
name: J Geyre

title: "absolutely lovely experience"

review: It was an absolutely lovely experience and the cabin crew were all very accommodating and excellent, I would like to personally thank Joseph and Tom for making our flight as comfortable as possible.

title: "spoiled by the ground staff at Doha"
name: Tony Halliwell
review: Wonderful experience spoiled by the ground staff at Doha. The flight from London Heathrow started out amazingly with the service at the premium check-in and passage through the security fast lane. The Qatar premium lounge at Heathrow is an oasis of calm and the food and drinks are excellent. Some of the staff lacked the polished 5 star touch. Service and comfort on our A380, although not the new q-suites, was excellent. Delicious food for breakfast and lunch. A stark contrast to the poor offerings on our outbound flight. The seats were comfortable. The Heathrow flight arrived at an A-Gate and the Dubai flight departed from C-gate. When we got to the C-gate we politely asked if there was a lounge close to the gate and received a very terse and rude no response from the Qatar Airways lady who was setting up the gate. Business/first passengers were made to hang around for 30 minutes whilst economy class passengers were bussed out to the plane. Not the service we paid for. Service on the flight to Dubai (A350) was good and the seats comfortable.
route: London Heathrow to Dubai via Doha

title: "Excellent experience let down by delays"
name: Tony Halliwell
review: Excellent experience let down by delays on the flight from Dubai which meant we didn't have chance to try the lounge in Doha and limited food options on the night flight to Gatwick. The food was little more than snacks and not the 5 star service we expected. The food was a stark contrast to the morning return journey from Heathrow. The food on that flight was amazing. The seats on the 777 to Doha were the old style 2-2-2 seats but that didn't matter on such a short flight. On the flight to Gatwick the seats were comfortable but sadly not the new qsuites.

title: "five star customer service"
name: K Mansour
review: Brilliant, five star customer service provided by Namvkwla, Tae Vi and Calvin onboard QR962 on 2nd October. Nothing was to much for them. Food was okay, seat was fine onboard the new A350-100.
route: London Heathrow to Denpasar Bali via Doha

title: "missing bag apparently was traced in AMS"
name: Philip Armstrong
review: It pains me to share this experience as I have always been a big fan of Qatar, having flown on it many times in my career while based in Washington DC and Geneva (business and first class). And, in fact, paid the extra money to upgrade to business class when I retired and returned to South Africa with my wife during the pandemic and it was expertly handled and a very satisfying, secure experience. Checked in at Schipol around noon on Tuesday 26 September travelling QR0274 to Doha, then QR1369 to Cape Town on Wednesday 27 September. Checked in three bags, two were received, and one was not. Reported at CPT on arrival on 27 September and a property irregularity report completed with appropriate supporting information including pics. Since then it has been a nightmare of correspondence and communication with various people in CPT, DOHA and AMS. All to NO avail. No one seems to have the capability, willingness or interest in helping a customer retrieve their missing bag containing valued personal possessions, including our family Holy Bible which is irreplaceable. Apparently the missing bag apparently was traced in AMS and was meant to be loaded on QR0274/29 September to be delivered in CPT on QR1369/30 September. Nothing arrived and there seems to be complete confusion between CPT and Qatar representatives in Doha and AMS. It seems AMS are not responding but I find it hard to believe that Qatar would not physically investigate what is going on at AMS with their baggage handlers, whom I believe are Swissport. I tried Swissport and the Station Manager in AMS but they don't answer their phones. I keep being assured that the bag is on the next flight but nothing. It is now a week since the missing bag was traced and it is just endless communication with people who want nothing to do with the problem and passing the blame to someone else. No one is willing to show any initiative or take accountability.
route: Amsterdam to Cape Town via Doha
